description: Comprehensive inventory of all data sources used in Housing Analytics. Each entry documents the file location, origin, refresh method, and fallback behavior.

sources.source_name: US Census Bureau TIGERweb — Colorado County Boundaries
sources.fallback_rule: Render map without county boundary overlay; log warning to console
sources.description: GeoJSON polygon boundaries for all 64 Colorado counties sourced from Census TIGERweb ArcGIS REST service. Used as the base layer for choropleth maps throughout the dashboards.

sources.source_name: HUD Low-Income Housing Tax Credit (LIHTC) Database
sources.fallback_rule: Display empty LIHTC layer with informational tooltip; hide allocations chart
sources.description: Point features representing LIHTC-funded affordable housing projects in Colorado, including unit counts, allocation year, and credit percentage. Seed data for 12 representative projects across 8 counties.

sources.source_name: US Census Bureau — American Community Survey (ACS) 5-Year Estimates, 2022
sources.fallback_rule: Show statewide ACS summary only; disable per-tract choropleth layer
sources.description: Census tract-level housing and economic indicators for Colorado, including renter/owner household counts, median gross rent, median household income, cost burden rate, and vacancy rate (15 tracts).

sources.source_name: US Census Bureau TIGERweb — Colorado Census Tract Centroids
sources.fallback_rule: Disable tract-level point clustering; fall back to county centroid display
sources.description: Latitude/longitude centroids for Colorado census tracts used for map point placement and spatial joins. Covers 22 representative tracts across 12 major counties.

sources.source_name: OpenStreetMap — Overpass API (Grocery/Supermarket nodes)
sources.fallback_rule: Hide grocery amenity layer on map; suppress amenity score calculation
sources.description: Point features for major grocery and supermarket locations in Colorado (20 stores) used to compute walkability and amenity proximity scores in the market analysis dashboards.

sources.source_name: OpenStreetMap — Overpass API (Healthcare facility nodes)
sources.fallback_rule: Hide healthcare amenity layer on map; suppress healthcare proximity scoring
sources.description: Point features for hospitals, clinics, and healthcare facilities in Colorado used to score healthcare access as part of the Housing Needs Assessment amenity index.

sources.source_name: OpenStreetMap — Overpass API (School nodes)
sources.fallback_rule: Hide schools amenity layer on map; suppress school proximity scoring
sources.description: Point features for K-12 schools in Colorado used to assess educational facility proximity as part of the Housing Needs Assessment amenity scoring model.

sources.source_name: OpenStreetMap — Overpass API (Retail node clusters)
sources.fallback_rule: Hide retail amenity layer on map; suppress retail proximity scoring
sources.description: Point features representing retail commercial nodes in Colorado used for walkability and amenity scoring in the market intelligence dashboards.

sources.source_name: Colorado Proposition 123 — DOLA Jurisdiction Opt-In Registry
sources.fallback_rule: Display all Colorado jurisdictions without Prop 123 compliance status overlay
sources.description: Registry of Colorado municipalities and counties that have opted into Proposition 123 affordable housing funding, with affordability commitment tier and unit targets.

sources.source_name: Colorado Housing and Finance Authority (CHFA) — LIHTC Allocation Data
sources.fallback_rule: Hide CHFA allocation chart; display statewide HUD totals only
sources.description: Annual LIHTC allocation data from CHFA including award amounts, project counts, and unit totals by year. Used to populate the LIHTC allocation trend charts on the dashboard.

sources.source_name: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis — FRED Economic Data
sources.fallback_rule: Use cached fred-data.json snapshot; display cache-age warning badge on charts
sources.description: Macroeconomic time-series data from FRED including mortgage rates, housing starts, and price indices. Fetched via Cloudflare Worker proxy and cached locally as fallback.

sources.source_name: US Census Bureau — ACS 5-Year County-Level Demographics
sources.fallback_rule: Show statewide Colorado demographics only; disable county-level breakdowns
sources.description: County-level demographic statistics for Colorado including population, housing units, tenure, and income distribution. Used in the Census and regional dashboards.

sources.source_name: Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) — Quarterly Census of Employment & Wages (QCEW)
sources.fallback_rule: Hide employment trend charts; display static summary table
sources.description: County-level employment and wage data for Colorado sourced from BLS QCEW, including industry sector breakdowns and wage band distributions used in the economic dashboard.

sources.source_name: HUD — Difficult Development Areas (DDA) Designations
sources.fallback_rule: Disable DDA overlay layer; suppress DDA bonus credit indicator
sources.description: HUD-designated Difficult Development Areas in Colorado where 30% LIHTC basis boost is available. Updated annually by HUD; used to flag eligible project locations on the LIHTC dashboard.

sources.source_name: HUD — Qualified Census Tracts (QCT) Designations
sources.fallback_rule: Disable QCT overlay layer; suppress QCT basis boost indicator
sources.description: HUD-designated Qualified Census Tracts in Colorado where 30% LIHTC basis boost is available due to low-income concentration. Used to flag eligible tracts on the LIHTC dashboard.

sources.source_name: HUD CHAS — Comprehensive Housing Affordability Strategy
sources.fallback_rule: Omit income-qualified demand sub-section; display coverage warning
sources.description: Income-qualified renter households by AMI tier (30/50/60/80%) at census tract level. Required for CHFA capture rate calculation. Released ~3 years lagged. Build via scripts/market/fetch_hud_chas.py.

sources.source_name: ACS B25070 — Gross Rent as % of Income by Tract
sources.fallback_rule: Use county-level GRAPI bins as proxy; display coverage warning
sources.description: Tract-level rent burden distribution (Gross Rent as % of Income). Currently only available at county level via GRAPI bins. Add to acs_tract_metrics_co.json via build_public_market_data.py. Requires CENSUS_API_KEY.

sources.source_name: ACS B25063 — Gross Rent Distribution by Tract
sources.fallback_rule: Omit actual rent distribution from capture rate calculations; use median rent estimate only
sources.description: Actual gross rent distributions by tract for capture rate calculations. Provides rent bands rather than median only, enabling better income-to-rent affordability matching. Requires CENSUS_API_KEY.

sources.source_name: DOLA Building Permit Data — Colorado New Construction Pipeline
sources.fallback_rule: Omit supply pipeline section; display placeholder in PMA tool
sources.description: New residential construction permits by jurisdiction (annual). Used to estimate supply pipeline competing with proposed LIHTC project. Excel download — no live API. Refresh annually.

sources.source_name: USPS Vacancy Data (HUD aggregated) — Quarterly Vacancy Rates
sources.fallback_rule: Fall back to ACS vacancy_rate field; display note that USPS vacancy unavailable
sources.description: HUD-aggregated USPS address vacancy data at tract and ZIP code level, updated quarterly. Provides more current vacancy estimates than the 5-year ACS. Used in PMA demand and absorption analysis.

sources.source_name: CHFA Rent & Income Limits — LIHTC AMI-Restricted Rent Limits
sources.fallback_rule: Use HUD published AMI limits as fallback for capture rate calculation
sources.description: CHFA-published AMI-restricted rent limits by bedroom type and county, updated annually when HUD releases new AMI figures. Required for achievable rent analysis in LIHTC market studies.
